newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. Today's -5.16% (close ~$105.53, intraday range $100.57-$115.57) is a flow-driven giveback of the AI-photonics sympathy rally: IPGP jumped ~5.8% on June 2 alongside MACOM/Amkor after Nvidia's Computex/GTC Taipei AI-infrastructure keynote, then added 3.6% on June 8. No earnings, contracts, M&A, capacity news, or June 8-9 analyst actions found. Standing backdrop: May 5 Q1 beat (book-to-bill >1 for a second straight quarter, Needham upgrade Hold->Buy, $10M Crossbow defense follow-on) offset by tariff-driven gross-margin compression (~37.5%) and a GuruFocus flag of ~31% above GF Value with $1.4M insider selling.

sources.label: StockStory - IPG Photonics, MACOM, and Amkor Shares Skyrocket (June 2 Nvidia AI-sympathy move)
GuruFocus - IPGP up 3.6% June 8 but ~31% above GF Value, insider selling
Simply Wall St - IPGP valuation check after Needham upgrade and AI infrastructure tailwinds

verdicts.rankRationale: Today's -5.2% is a flow-driven retrace of the Computex AI-sympathy pop, not damage to the latent industrial-photonics thesis, so the rank-12 placement from yesterday's hold stands; the pullback marginally improves entry asymmetry without adding new evidence.
verdicts.bull: Book-to-bill above 1 for two straight quarters, the Needham upgrade, and the Crossbow defense follow-on show real demand inflection; any concrete AI-datacenter or advanced-manufacturing design win would re-rate the latent-node case from a now-cheaper base.
verdicts.neutral: Stock is still +41% YTD on AI-narrative beta with no disclosed AI revenue; the latent thesis remains optionality, not evidence, and today's noise does not change its position.
verdicts.bear: Five-year sales declined ~4% annually, tariffs compressed Q1 gross margin ~2pts, and insider selling at ~31% above GF Value means the industrial laser cycle, not AI, sets the floor if the narrative bid fades.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Industrial capex recovery or a named AI/photonics manufacturing design win that converts optionality into revenue.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Industrial laser cycle softness plus tariff margin drag while the AI-sympathy premium deflates.

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already moved IPGP down for the YTD rerating without a direct CPO revenue path; today's flow-driven -5.2% partially relieves that crowding penalty but brings zero new CPO evidence, so no second consecutive move at rank 37.
verdicts.bull: A 5% washout on no fundamental news trims the chase premium on a genuine photonics franchise that could be pulled into data-center optics if CPO supply chains broaden beyond COHR/LITE/MTSI.
verdicts.neutral: The CPO revenue path remains indirect and unproven; IPGP keeps trading in sympathy with the optics complex on Nvidia headlines, and the setup is unchanged from yesterday.
verdicts.bear: It is an industrial-cycle laser company wearing an AI-photonics multiple; the June 2 rally was Computex beta, not CPO content, so when narrative flows rotate, IPGP de-rates without ever having had the revenue.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: A disclosed datacenter/CPO-adjacent component order or supply agreement that makes the AI revenue path direct.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded AI-narrative flow money rotates out of indirect names first, and IPGP is among the most indirect on this list.
